_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Transrectal Ultrasound guided prostate biopsy (minimum 12 core)
clinical_indications: Elevated prostate-specific antigen (PSA), High Gleason score.
Adenocarcinoma, Brachytherapy for prostate cancer, Cryotherapy for prostate cancer.
Deroofing or aspiration of ejaculatory ducts, prostatic cysts, or prostatic abscesses.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure
USG with prostate size and Post Void Volume establishing suspicion of malignancy

claim.label: Histopathology showing reporting of minimum 12 core samples of prostate. (As applicable)

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure submitted?
Was the USG report with prostate size and Post Void Volume establishing suspicion of malignancy submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Histopathology showing reporting of minimum 12 core samples of prostate submitted? (As applicable)
Was the Detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes lacking evaluation findings, indication for procedure, or planned line of management.
USG report not establishing suspicion of malignancy or missing prostate size/Post Void Volume details.
Histopathology report for prostate not showing minimum 12 core samples or not submitted.
Detailed indoor case papers not available or incomplete.
Discharge summary missing or follow-up date not submitted.

clinical_key_pointers: The precise way to find the cancer cell inside prostate gland is the surgical removal and histopathological examination of the entire gland is called the transrectal ultrasound (TRUS)-guided systematic biopsy.
One of the major limitations with the procedure is the amount of tissue sampled during prostate biopsy is limited and cancer cells can be missed
The 12-core extended systematic transrectal ultrasound (TRUS)-guided biopsy (TRUS-Gb) is currently an accepted standard method to detect tumors' Gleason grades.
